items.title: Heated Rivalry, Off Campus driving ice hockey boom in Canberra

children.content: Women now outnumber men as attendees to Canberra Brave ice hockey games — thanks in part to a surge in popularity for the sport inspired by the recent shows Heated Rivalry and Off Campus.

Ella Bright and Belmont Cameli intimately close in a lecture theatre in a scene from Off Campus.
People play ice hockey on a rink in a darkened stadium.

items.title: A man fled a 'bounty' in Canberra. Months later, he was dead in Brisbane

children.content: Neil Tracey's death was ruled an overdose in what investigators would call an open and shut case. His family are questioning if police knew about the bounty that led him to flee his hometown.

image.altText: Graphic of a man's smiling face with newspapers articles surrounding it.

items.title: Court hears of 'tragic mistake' that caused fatal Canberra crash

children.content: The daughters of a motorcyclist killed in Canberra's north tried to reach him for their nightly phone call as he lay dying on the road, an ACT court has heard.

image.altText: A man with a beard smiles, wearing a hat that says Harley Davidson, sitting inside an eatery.

items.title: Budget blowout in Queanbeyan with 100yo pipe found in unexpected spot

children.content: A century-old stormwater drain nearly 2 metres thick is uncovered beneath Queanbeyan's town centre, forcing the council to extend major roadworks and source extra funding.

items.title: ACT education minister censured in Assembly as schools close for strike

children.content: ACT MLA Yvette Berry is censured in the Legislative Assembly over her record as minister for housing and education amid a teachers' strike that has closed schools across the territory.

items.title: Parton says Canberrans are 'more vulnerable' after budget's release

children.content: The Canberra Liberals and the ACT Greens issue their responses to the ACT budget, criticising the government's fiscal plan, principally around cost of living, transport and overall management of the territory's finances.

articles.title: Canberrans looking for cost-of-living relief in ACT budget might be left wanting

children.content: During practice at this Canberra football club, players discuss the ways they are saving money. But if they were looking for cost-of-living relief in today's ACT budget, they might not have found what they were looking for.

image.altText: Rach stands outside at night holding a football on the field, wearing a beanie.

articles.title: Trump certainly isn't helping the ACT's homegrown budget deterioration

items.title: Quick hits: Legends stunned by on-air gaffe, Trump's call goes cold

children.content: The US and Canada hit the pitch for the first time at their home World Cup, but on day two the drama extended from the field to the TV booths and beyond. Here are the quick hits.

articles.title: US romps to 4-1 victory over Paraguay in electric World Cup opener

children.content: World Cup co-host USA gets its campaign underway with a dominant 4-1 thrashing of Paraguay after Canada rescued a late draw with Bosnia and Herzegovina in Toronto.

articles.title: 'A footballer's dream': From humble beginnings to the world stage

children.content: The Touré brothers snuck into their local football field after hours to play the game they loved, while the Yengi brothers kicked around makeshift balls made of plastic bags. Two of them have now made it onto the world stage as Socceroos.

image.altText: composite of a Mo Toure as a young boy in a soccer field and him as an adult in a Socceroos guernsey

articles.title: How far can the Socceroos really go at the FIFA World Cup?

items.title: The sword that links feared pirates to Britain's greatest naval hero
